title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R package (site) converts parameterized “site files” (sub-nationally disaggregated malaria data) malariasimulation model input parameter lists. Part malariaverse ecosystem.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-badge.R")  # Full package check (includes tests, examples, vignettes) devtools::check()  # Rebuild documentation after modifying roxygen comments devtools::document()  # Build pkgdown site locally pkgdown::build_site()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: site_parameters() R/site_parameters.R main entry point. takes site file components (interventions, demography, vectors, seasonality, EIR) year range, orchestrates calls intervention-specific modules build complete malariasimulation parameter list.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: intervention type file adds parameters simulation: - R/itns.R - Insecticide-treated nets (uses netz package distribution modeling) - R/irs.R - Indoor residual spraying - R/smc.R - Seasonal malaria chemoprevention - R/pmc.R - Perennial malaria chemoprevention - R/vaccines.R - Malaria vaccines - R/treatment.R - Treatment/case management - R/lsm.R - Larval source management composed R/interventions.R calls module sequence.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/time.R - Year--timestep conversion (calendar_to_timestep(), calculate_total_timesteps()) R/seasonality.R, R/vectors.R, R/demography.R - Set baseline model parameters site data R/single_site.R - subset_site() filters site files country/ISO/name R/fetch.R - Downloads site files malariaverse Packit server via orderly R/badge.R - Data provenance badge functions tracking data sources

title: CLAUDE.md
text: plot_site_diagnostic() - Composite 6-panel dashboard (map, prevalence, interventions, vectors, resistance, age distribution) assembled patchwork plot_interventions() / plot_site_interventions() - Multi-layer intervention timeline (ITN, SMC, IRS, treatment, vaccines, PMC, LSM, rainfall) Individual plots: plot_site_prevalence(), plot_vector_species(), plot_pyrethroid_resistance(), plot_age_distribution_stacked(), plot_site_map() Theme/palette helpers: theme_site(), site_age_palette(), site_vector_palette(), default_intervention_colours() Internal prepare_* helpers handle data wrangling per intervention type plotting

title: CLAUDE.md
text: data/net_efficacy.rda data/irs_efficacy.rda - Lookup tables intervention efficacy parameters Source scripts data-raw/

title: CLAUDE.md
text: malariasimulation (mrc-ide/malariasimulation@dev) - simulation engine netz (mrc-ide/netz@site-2601) - Net distribution modelling orderly - Data pipeline/reproducibility framework fetching site files ggplot2 / patchwork - Plotting multi-panel composition (R/plot.R)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: changes, run devtools::test() verify. Use devtools::check() larger changes (new exports, vignette edits, dependency changes). Always ask committing — don’t auto-commit. Assume deep R knowledge; need explain standard R idioms package development concepts.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Prefer clear, informative error messages. Use cli::cli_abort() cli::cli_warn() user-facing errors/warnings formatting helps (e.g., named bullets, inline markup variable names/values). Use standard stop() / warning() simple internal errors cli formatting adds value. Validate inputs public function boundaries; internal helpers can trust callers.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: man/ — auto-generated devtools::document(). Edit roxygen2 comments R/ source files instead.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Follow tidyverse style conventions (e.g., pipe-friendly functions, tidy evaluation appropriate) Prioritise clarity readability cleverness Use base pipe |> magrittr %>% Use snake_case names (functions, variables, arguments, file names) New functions must include roxygen2 documentation corresponding unit tests

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Uses testthat edition 3. Tests tests/testthat/ snapshot tests tests/testthat/_snaps/. Helper functions creating test fixtures R/test-helpers.R (e.g., create_example_site()).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: GitHub Actions runs R CMD check macOS, Windows, Ubuntu (multiple R versions). Coverage via codecov push main.

title: Accessing site-files
text: access site file - collated set data specific country - need follow steps: Sign GitHub account, single user accounts free. Send email malariaverse@imperial.ac.uk ask site file access. Please include following details: name institution GitHub username short description project use site files added malariaverse GitHub team. receive confirmation (look notification GitHub), can use site package built-functionality download site files. can call fetch_site(), specifying ISO3c country code country interest. example, Nigeria: fetch_site(iso3c = "NGA", admin_level = 1, urban_rural = TRUE). first time , see interactive authentication instructions asking access GitHub link providing authentication code. Open link copy code authorise access. process cache credentials don’t authorise every time. Please make sure installed orderly r-universe:

title: Accessing site-files
text: Site files periodically updated. can check version inspecting site metadata. function available_sites() lists site files currently available server. default, fetch_site(iso3c = ...) downloads latest version site file. reproducibility, can also specify admin_level, urban_rural, version fetch particular file. stable workflows, recommend downloading storing site files use , rather always pulling latest version. Otherwise, downstream pipelines may break new site file options become available. News new updates posted .

title: Accessing site-files
text: encounter issues process, please post issue, much detail possible site package issue page.

title: Interventions

text: net_type Type netdistribution_type Type net distribution, mass-campaign routinedistribution_day_of_year Day year distributiondistribution_lower Lower bound possible itn_input_distdistribution_upper Upper bound possible itn_input_dist

title: Interventions
text: Supported net types currently include: “pyrethroid_only”, “pyrethroid_pbo”, “pyrethroid_pyrrole”. Net type introductions informed data Alliance Malaria Prevention. Current available net types : pyrethroid_only, pyrethroid_pbo pyrethroid_pyrrole. Absent systematic, country data mass campaign dates locations assume mass campaigns occur first day year routine distributions occur quarterly. Lower upper bounds help ensure sensible model input distributions estimated, see stop_missing_itn_input_dist() full details. Due differences availability data sources approach countries within sub-Saharan Africa differs countries outside sub-Saharan Africa: Within sub-Saharan Africa: population risk weighted mean ITN use estimates site taken malaria atlas project. Outside sub-Saharan Africa: ITN use much heterogeneous outside SSA data less systematically collected. result, strong assumptions associated historical scale magnitude ITN distributions. make assumption reported ITN distributions (detailed world malaria report) targeted areas highest baseline prevalence prioritised first. Net use implemented maximum usage 55% area, sequentially working targeting reported total number bed nets allocated. Available data ITN use extend present year. Missing ITN use estimates present filled assuming constant, continuing level coverage. respect multi-year cyclical nature ITN distribution cycles missing estimates filled assuming coverage constant respect 3 years prior. example years 2024, 2025 2026 missing 2024 == 2021, 2025 == 2022 2026 == 2023. ⚠️ Warning: ITNs work passed site::site_parameters() need specify column: site_file$interventions$itn$implementation$itn_input_dist indicating size model ITN distributions year. ITN usage. Use site::site_usage_to_model_distribution() convert usage model input distributions, call stop_missing_itn_input_dist() full details. Given ITN type level pyrethroid insecticide resistance, site package link corresponding estimates key ITN efficacy parameters.

text: site_file$interventions$irs$implementation IRS implementation details

title: Interventions
text: irs_cov IRS coveragepeak_season Rainfall seasonal peak day yearinsecticide IRS insecticideround Spray roundspray_day_of_year Day year spray round

title: Interventions
text: ITNs, due differences availability data sources approach countries within sub-Saharan Africa differs countries outside sub-Saharan Africa: Within sub-Saharan Africa: population risk weighted mean IRS coverage estimates spatial unit summarised malaria atlas project Outside sub-Saharan Africa: IRS coverage much heterogeneous outside SSA data less systematically collected. result, strong assumptions associated historical scale magnitude IRS campaigns. make assumption reported persons protected IRS (detailed world malaria report) targeted areas highest baseline prevalence prioritised first. IRS coverage implemented maximum usage 80% area, sequentially working targeting reported total number persons protected allocated. assumed DDT-type insecticide used prior 2017, switch actellic-like insecticide. Current available IRS insecticide options : “ddt”, “actellic”, “bendiocarb” “sumishield”. assume single IRS spray round per year. Given IRS type level pyrethroid insecticide resistance, site package link corresponding estimates key ITN efficacy parameters. Available data IRS coverage (via MAP world malaria report) extend present year. Missing IRS coverage estimates present filled assuming constant, continuing level coverage.

title: Latest changes to malariaverse
text: page describes significant update malariaverse site files several supporting packages. changes affect — cases break — existing workflows, please read analyses built previous site files packages.

title: Latest changes to malariaverse
text: malariaverse packages site files substantially updated (full detail site PR #56 postie PR #8). headline changes include: 🌍 Population demography methods updated latest WorldPop outputs. 🌧️ Monthly rainfall estimates updated CHIRPS v3.0. 🗺️ Updated latest MAP rasters. 📊 figures updated 2025 World Malaria Report. 💉 Updated UNICEF malaria vaccine dose-delivery data. ✨ New functionality: option specify parameter draw, Plasmodium vivax model, within-year (sub-year) intervention specification. 🛏️ Refined intervention implementation — net modelling now aligns closely MAP net model, IRS trial--implementation downscaling, corrected SMC/PMC round timing. ⚠️ itn_model_dist now required input (helper functions detailed error message guide ’s missing). 🏥 previous fixed shortcut (malaria deaths taken constant multiple hospitalised severe incidence) replaced explicit calculation splits severe cases hospital community streams, case-fatality ratio. 🏥 postie, new ft_sev input lets set vary time proportion severe cases reach hospital. defaults 0.8, reproduces previous behaviour. 🧾 New disaggregated output columns (hospital vs community severe streams) case-fatality-ratio arguments available. Importantly, alongside data methods updates, structure site files changed, supporting malariaverse packages updated step. Taken together, means code written previous site files packages likely run unchanged updated versions.

title: Latest changes to malariaverse
text: Many need re-run reproduce earlier analyses. Two things make possible: Older site files remain available fetch_site(), can continue retrieve previous data. frozen previous versions malariaverse packages coordinated snapshot tag malariaverse_01_2025. Pairing older site files tagged set packages reproduces environment existing workflows built . install frozen package set, e.g. pak: Remember, can see available site-files versions calling site::available_sites()

title: Latest changes to malariaverse
text: changes reflected latest versions malariaverse packages site files. analyses rely previous behaviour, ’d recommend noting package versions using (installing tagged snapshot ) updating.

title: Latest changes to malariaverse
text: questions run problems, please open issue site package issue page contact us malariaverse@imperial.ac.uk. 🙏 Huge thanks many individuals contributed changes.

Finer files add successive sub-national levels: name_1, possibly name_2, name_3, . urban_rural present urban/rural split applied. brevity key columns repeated per-table variable lists elsewhere documentation.

Pixels assigned “urban” descending order population density implied national urban population total year reached. Remaining pixels classified rural. Population risk variables defined population areas Plasmodium falciparum, Plasmodium vivax either prevalence >0% year 2000.

title: Mosquito Vectors
text: Within sub-Saharan Africa: countries sub-Saharan Africa statistical model relative abundance major vectors produced Sinka et al. countries extract relative abundance arabiensis, funestus gambiae using malaria atlas project “Anopheles arabiensis Patton, 1905”, “Anopheles funestus” “Anopheles gambiae Giles, 1902” rasters. Proportions normalised sum one (species may present data). Outside sub-Saharan Africa: countries outside sub-Saharan Africa estimates relative abundance vector species. however probability occurrence maps large number species Sinka et al. locations select maximum three species ranked average probability occurrence across site. species given equally weighted relative abundance. bionomics parameters vectors sub-Saharan Africa parameterised using fitted parameters malariasimulation. vectors parameterised (unpublished) literature review Arran Hamlet. unknown species assigned median vector bionomics parameters across species site. Please contact Pete Winskill directly using estimates. blood_meal_rates, foraging_time mum parameters assumed fixed previously fitted values.

title: Site specific malariasimulation modelling
text: malariaverse site files several supporting packages significant update changes site-file structure may break existing workflows. Please read Latest changes malariaverse ’s changed keep reproducing existing work. site package supports malariaverse users : Access download latest country site-files 🌐 ➡️ 💻 Translate site file information malariasimulation parameters 🌍 ➡️ 📉 ⚠️ site package streamlines model runs specific geographies, also encapsulates many underlying assumptions, limitations, uncertainties. Mis-specifying inputs misinterpreting outputs complex malariasimulation model remains possible. strongly recommend consulting member Imperial College modelling team using results science, policy decision-making purposes. ⚠️ every effort made ensure reliability package outputs, authors, contributors, affiliated institutions accept responsibility liability errors, omissions, consequences arising use. results interpreted caution professional judgement. Email: malariaverse@imperial.ac.uk

title: Site specific malariasimulation modelling
text: site-file file storing sub nationally disaggregated information country. Components site-file include: Metadata, high level information country, site-file version spatial boundaries. Historical Epidemiological Data estimates cases, deaths parasite prevalence, calibrated baseline EIR bias-correction factors. Population Demography information. Estimates Historical Intervention Use country. Rainfalll Seasonality data. Information Mosquito Vectors. Blood Disorders Accessibility, inherited blood-disorder frequencies travel-time accessibility metrics.

title: Site specific malariasimulation modelling
text: Data inputs linked throughout via data buttons link information data sources.

title: Site specific malariasimulation modelling
text: Please see detailed guide information gain access download site files. using site, use acknowledge cite sources.

what: Translating site-file information to malariasimulation parameters
title: Site specific malariasimulation modelling
text: access site file, need run model Isolate single site, single sub-national unit within country Create parameter list Pass malariasimulation run simulation
code: # Pull information for a single sub-national unit from the site-file site <- create_example_site(itn_use = 0)  # Convert site information to malariasimulation parameters site_par <- site_parameters(   interventions = site$interventions,   demography = site$demography,   vectors = site$vectors,   seasonality = site$seasonality,   eir = site$eir$eir,   overrides = list(     human_population = 1000   ),   start_year = 2000,   end_year = 2002 )  # Run the model! site_sim <- malariasimulation::run_simulation(   timesteps = site_par$timesteps,   parameters = site_par )

title: Site specific malariasimulation modelling

title: Calculate total model timesteps — calculate_total_timesteps
text: Calculate total model timesteps

title: Calculate total model timesteps — calculate_total_timesteps
text: start_year Numeric scalar. First year model period end_year Numeric scalar. Last year model period (inclusive)

title: Calculate total model timesteps — calculate_total_timesteps
text: Numeric scalar. Total number timesteps (days) model run

title: Calculate total model timesteps — calculate_total_timesteps
text: Total runtime = (end_year - start_year + 1) years. years treated 365 days (leap year adjustment).

title: Calculate total model timesteps — calculate_total_timesteps
code: # Data from 2000-2020 calculate_total_timesteps(start_year = 2000, end_year = 2020) #> [1] 7665 # Returns: 7665 (21 years * 365 days)  # Short period calculate_total_timesteps(start_year = 2000, end_year = 2005) #> [1] 2190 # Returns: 2190 (6 years * 365 days)

text: year Numeric vector years (e.g., c(2000, 2001, 2005)) day_of_year Numeric vector day within year (1-365) start_year Numeric scalar indicating model start year

title: Convert calendar dates to model timesteps — calendar_to_timestep
text: Numeric vector model timesteps days since model start

title: Convert calendar dates to model timesteps — calendar_to_timestep
text: Model timestep 1 corresponds January 1st start_year. years treated 365 days (leap year adjustment).

title: Convert calendar dates to model timesteps — calendar_to_timestep
code: # Model starts in 1980, interventions begin in 2000 calendar_to_timestep(year = 2000, day_of_year = 1, start_year = 1980) #> [1] 7301 # Returns: 7306 (20 years * 365 days + 1)  # Multiple dates calendar_to_timestep(   year = c(2000, 2001, 2005),   day_of_year = c(1, 100, 200),   start_year = 1980 ) #> [1] 7301 7765 9325

title: Create vaccine intervention examples — create_example_vaccine
text: vaccine Character. Vaccine type: "rtss", "r21", "rtss_r21" delivery Character. Delivery method: "age-based" "hybrid" boosters Character. Booster schedule: "none", "single", "multiple" primary_cov Numeric. Primary vaccination coverage (0-1). Default: 0.8 booster_cov Numeric. Booster vaccination coverage (0-1). Default: 0.4 years Numeric vector. Years implementation. Default: 2000:2002 name Character. Location name. Default: "place" peak_season Numeric. Peak transmission day year. Default: 100 primary_schedule Numeric vector. Primary vaccination ages days. Default: c(180, 210, 240)

title: Fetch a site file for a country from the malariaverse site file server. — fetch_site
text: site file identified country code, optionally admin_level, urban/rural setting version site files. latest packet server matching parameters used. default, calling `fetch_site()` `iso3c` downloads latest available version. reproducibility stable workflows, strongly recommended specify `version`, `admin_level`, `urban_rural` explicitly, download store site files locally. Otherwise, downstream pipelines may break new site file versions released.

title: Convert model input distributions to expected ITN usage — site_model_distribution_to_usage
text: distribution Numeric vector model input distribution values. usage_year Integer vector years estimate usage. usage_day_of_year Integer vector days year estimate usage. distribution_year Integer vector years distribution time points. distribution_day_of_year Integer vector days year distribution time points. net_loss_function Function describing net loss time. Defaults [netz::net_loss_map]. ... Additional arguments passed net_loss_function (e.g. `half_life`).

title: Create site parameters — site_parameters
text: Create site parameters

title: Create site parameters — site_parameters
code: site_parameters(   interventions,   demography,   vectors,   seasonality,   age_group = c(0, 5, 15, 100) * 365,   clinical_incidence = c(0, 5, 15, 100) * 365,   severe_incidence = c(0, 5, 15, 100) * 365,   prevalence = c(2, 10) * 365,   parasite = "falciparum",   eir = NULL,   draw = NULL,   overrides = list(),   start_year,   end_year,   irs_adjust = 0.75 )

title: Create site parameters — site_parameters
text: interventions Site intervention inputs demography Site demography inputs vectors Site vectors inputs seasonality Site seasonality inputs age_group Age breaks population size outputs days; default = c(0, 5, 15, 100) * 365 clinical_incidence Age breaks clinical incidence outputs days; default = c(0, 5, 15, 100) * 365 severe_incidence Age breaks severe incidence outputs days (pf ); default = c(0, 5, 15, 100) * 365 prevalence Age breaks prevalence outputs days; default = c(2, 10) * 365 parasite Can "falciparum" "vivax". vivax, SMC, RTSS PMC implemented eir Site baseline EIR draw malariasimulation parameter draw. Default NULL best-fit parameter set overrides List malariasimulation default parameter overrides start_year year simulation starts. inclusive burn-(recommended). example, site file data started year 2000 wanted specify 20 year burn-set start_year 1980. Interventions prior site file start year assumed 0 coverage, demography set profile first site file entry. end_year end year (inclusive) simulation irs_adjust scalar adjustment IRS coverage inputs account observed discrepancies IRS impact trial versus real-world implementation.

title: Create site parameters — site_parameters
text: malariasimulation parameter list

title: Convert ITN usage to model input distributions — site_usage_to_model_distribution
code: site_usage_to_model_distribution(   usage,   usage_year,   usage_day_of_year,   distribution_year,   distribution_day_of_year,   distribution_lower = NULL,   distribution_upper = NULL,   net_loss_function = netz::net_loss_map,   ... )

title: Convert ITN usage to model input distributions — site_usage_to_model_distribution
text: usage Numeric vector ITN usage values. usage_year Integer vector years corresponding `usage`. usage_day_of_year Integer vector days year corresponding `usage`. distribution_year Integer vector years distribution time points. distribution_day_of_year Integer vector days year distribution time points. distribution_lower Numeric vector lower bounds possible distributions. Defaults 0 distribution time point. distribution_upper Numeric vector upper bounds possible distributions. Defaults 1 distribution time point. net_loss_function Function describing net loss time. Defaults [netz::net_loss_map]. ... Additional arguments passed net_loss_function (e.g. `half_life`).

title: Convert ITN usage to model input distributions — site_usage_to_model_distribution
text: Numeric vector model input distribution values, one per   distribution time point.

title: site 2.0.0
text: malariaverse_01_2026

title: site 2.0.0
text: second full release site files, include latest (2025) World Malaria Report data. Update population distributions using new WorldPop rasters. Update rainfall data new CHIRPS v3.0. Update Malaria Atlas Project (MAP) P. falciparum P. Vivax prevalence. ITN usage (Africa), IRS coverage (Africa) effective treatment coverage input rasters latest release (08/2025). Note: new effective treatment covearge maps country-level constitutes reduction spatial resolution input many country-year combinations (reason unknown). Update malaria vaccine doses delivered data UNICEF. Note: still clear data-source sub-national targeted vaccine-type delivered doses. possible, also include specific primary dose scheduling delivery mode country vaccination. Additional specific flexibility implementation interventions. includes modular, granular breakdown intervention implmentation site_file$interventions. example, specific days year SMC rounds IRS rounds, flexibility specify annual mass quarterly routine bednet distributions etc. Plasmodium vivax implementation now runs vivax-specific version malariasimulation. Switch use MAP-style logistic retention curve bed nets. replaces previous exponential retention curve. flexible specification age ranges rates outputs. Documentation updated include descriptions every element site-file, better tracking data inputs used. Includes default parameterisation convert IRS trial impact implementation impact.

title: site 2.0.0
text: Public functions consume site file (e.g. site_parameters(), subset_site(), plot_* functions) now detect older site-file structure supplied abort informative message linking upgrade guide.

title: site 1.0.5
text: malariaverse_01_2025

title: site 1.0.5
text: first full release site files, include latest World Malaria Report 2024 data Update recent available intervention, burden boundary data Overhaul site file creation access use orderly2 packit Empirically-driven move longer (5 years) assumed net retention half life country specific, shorter retentions Inclusion age-disaggregated population estimates Inclusion boundary geometry Inclusion historical, monthly rainfall data Inclusion blood disorder data Inclusion travel time data Foundation vaccine (RTS,S R21) input monitoring

title: site 1.0.5
text: Fixed demography bug led misspecified population age-structure Fixed insecticide resistance half life bug
